name: Captum (PyTorch Explainability)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD 3-Clause); Meta/PyTorch project; no API costs

performance.notes: Integrated Gradients runs in seconds per sample on GPU; scales with model size

agent_readiness.notes: Meta's model interpretability library built on PyTorch. Algorithms: Integrated Gradients, GradCAM, LIME, DeepLIFT, SHAP, Feature Ablation, Guided Backpropagation, Occlusion. Works with any PyTorch model. Insights module for testing model interpretations. LLM attribution for explaining language model predictions. Essential for debugging deep learning models and vision/NLP explanations.

gotchas: Integrated Gradients slow for large inputs
Attribution methods make assumptions — results can be misleading
Requires access to model internals (not black-box)
best_when: Explaining PyTorch neural network predictions (vision, NLP)
Debugging model behavior in deep learning pipelines
LLM token attribution for understanding which inputs drive outputs
Research on model interpretability in deep learning
avoid_when: Tree-based models (SHAP TreeExplainer faster and more accurate)
Production serving explanations (too slow for real-time)
summary: Captum is Meta's model interpretability library for PyTorch. Implements Integrated Gradients, GradCAM, LIME, DeepLIFT, and other attribution methods. Works with any PyTorch model including LLMs for token attribution. Best for debugging and explaining deep learning models. PyTorch-only; for tree models, SHAP's TreeExplainer is faster and more accurate.
